content:
# recommendations — for the iter-036 plan agent

## Closest-to-completion / next move (1.1.3 `H_tensor`)
The substrate is landed and axiom-clean. The lane now needs a **two-step continuation**, in this order (prover's own handoff, endorsed):

1. **STEP 2.5 — re-type to functorial form FIRST.** Promote `External.pullbackTensorComparison`
   (object-level Iso) → a **NatIso of bifunctors** (`A,B ↦ f^*A ⊗ f^*B` vs `A,B ↦ f^*(A⊗B)`),
   exactly the @031→@032 re-type pattern that unblocked 1.1.2 (net axiom count UNCHANGED — object
   iso = `.app`). Then re-type `htensorComparison` θ to a **NatTrans** of the two functors
   `N ↦ H(I ⊗_S N, F)` and `N ↦ H(I,F) ⊗ N`. Without this, STEP 3 has nothing to feed Eilenberg–Watts.

2. **STEP 3 — decide the SECOND anchor form, then close.** The iso `H_tensor` = `θ.symm`. Two routes
   (planner must PICK one — this is the strategy fork the prover flagged, NOT a math question):
   - **(a) General categorical anchor (recommended):** "two right-exact, ⊕-preserving additive
     functors `S.Modules → S.Modules` that agree on `𝒪_S` (and respect the comparison) are naturally
     iso" — Eilenberg–Watts-style. This is a TRUE general fact, NOT a §1 restatement, parallel to the
     existing comparison anchors. Mathlib has no Eilenberg–Watts (loogle-confirmed @035), so anchor it.
   - **(b) Affine reduction + Watts on `Mod-A`:** reuse the already-anchored H base-change compat;
     requires the (PAUSED) H base-change machinery, heavier.
   - Then θ_{𝒪_S}=id (sanity-passed on paper) + EW ⇒ θ iso for all N ⇒ `⟨(htensorComparison …).symm via isIso⟩`.

## DO NOT retry without a structural change
- **Do NOT** attempt STEP 3 with only the current single anchor — the prover correctly stopped here;
  PROGRESS.md's one-anchor gate held. The blocker is a *decision* (which 2nd anchor), not a tactic to grind.
- **Do NOT** route θ_{𝒪_S}=id through `f^*𝒪_S ≅ 𝒪_X` — that iso is a KNOWN absent fact for
  `Scheme.Modules.pullback` (KB GAP, also blocked 1.4 `p*𝒪≅𝒪`). It is not an axiom-clean partial win.

## Blueprint / structural (from blueprint-doctor)
- **FIXED this review:** undefined macro `\tensorBC` (used `\tensorBC(f,I,N)` at chapter L1798/1802,
  never defined) → added `\providecommand{\tensorBC}{\operatorname{tensorBC}}` to `macros/common.tex`.
  The next `leanblueprint web` would have RecursionError-crashed without it. Planner: when authoring
  prose with a new `\macro(...)`, define the macro in `macros/common.tex` in the SAME iter (this is the
  2nd time — cf. @033 `\PresheafOfModules`/`\SheafOfModules`/`\coyoneda`).
- Coverage debt: **0 unmatched, 0 gaps** — planner's pre-authored `lem:pullbackTensorComparison` +
  `def:htensorComparison` blocks already cover the 2 new decls with correct `\lean{...}` pins. Clean.

## Off-lane (do not assign)
- `ext_finite_flat` (1.10) — infra-blocked (`IsLFPAndFlatOn` opaque; needs `R^q f_*` + internal `ℰxt`,
  the PAUSED internal-ℰxt strand). Keep deferred.

## Reusable pattern (record)
- **corepr-inverse map-builder:** to build a map INTO a non-representing object `W` from a universal
  element `e`, use `(H_represents … ).some.inv.app W e`; no `show`/coercion needed when the
  `tensorBC`/`tensorMod` defeqs hold (type-checked first try @035). Generalizes the 1.1.2 coyoneda close.
